benchmark_report.title: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKING REPORT
benchmark_report.subtitle: Scientific Measurement and Brutal Judgment
benchmark_report.methodology: Ruthless statistical analysis with evidence-based roasting

executive_summary.verdict: These commands have more layers than a wedding cake and about as much substance as cotton candy

ultrathink.md.savage_judgment: This isn't thinking—it's thinking about thinking about thinking. 357 lines to ask 'what do you want me to do?' That's not intelligence, that's bureaucracy with XML tags.
ultrathink.md.evidence: Contains 15 nested thinking blocks and asks for interactive clarification like a GPS that lost signal. The metacognitive monitoring section alone is 20 lines of navel-gazing.
ultrathink.md.improvement_needed: Cut 70% of the meta-thinking. Users want solutions, not philosophical dissertations.

adhd-morning-assistant.md.savage_judgment: 309 lines to plan a morning? My grandmother could write a better morning routine on a napkin. This reads like a startup pitch deck had a baby with a self-help book.
adhd-morning-assistant.md.evidence: Includes JavaScript code blocks in Markdown that will never execute. Claims to be 'AI-powered' but requires more manual setup than assembling IKEA furniture.
adhd-morning-assistant.md.improvement_needed: Actually implement the AI features instead of describing them in flowery language. Less poetry, more productivity.

reasoning-wrapper.md.savage_judgment: A wrapper that wraps wrappers while thinking about wrapping. This is like Russian nesting dolls designed by someone who never learned when to stop. 415 lines to add reasoning? The only reasoning needed is 'Why does this exist?'
reasoning-wrapper.md.evidence: Claims to be able to wrap itself recursively. Features 11 different thinking blocks and 4 wrapper templates. The meta-capabilities section reads like a feature list for a product that doesn't work.
reasoning-wrapper.md.improvement_needed: Delete 80% of this. If you need 415 lines to add 'reasoning' to something, the reasoning is already broken.

safe-code-beautifier.md.savage_judgment: 427 lines to make code pretty? This has more safety checks than a nuclear power plant. By the time this finishes 'safely' beautifying your code, you could have rewritten the entire application.
safe-code-beautifier.md.evidence: 17 different safety checks and 8 validation thinking blocks. The error handling section is longer than most functions. Claims 'zero behavior change' but takes longer to analyze than most refactoring sessions.
safe-code-beautifier.md.improvement_needed: Trust the user to have version control. Less bubble wrap, more actual beautification.

senior-developer-analysis.md.savage_judgment: This command has more layers than a senior developer's experience and twice the ego. 390 lines to explain code? Real senior developers explain complex systems in 5 sentences, not 5 pages.
senior-developer-analysis.md.evidence: Features 14 references to 'senior experience' and 7 different thinking frameworks. The 'mentoring-focused communication' section could mentor an entire university.
senior-developer-analysis.md.improvement_needed: Channel actual senior developer energy: be concise, direct, and stop mansplaining every design pattern.

primary_issues: Thinking-block addiction: Every command has 8+ thinking sections
Template obsession: More templates than actual functionality
Meta-meta-meta thinking: Thinking about thinking about thinking
Complexity worship: Confusing complicated with sophisticated
XML ceremony: More angle brackets than actual logic

immediate_actions: SLASH AND BURN: Remove 60-80% of thinking blocks
SIMPLIFY: Each command should do ONE thing well
FOCUS: Stop building frameworks, start solving problems
architectural_fixes: Replace XML thinking orchestration with simple functions
Convert template systems to direct implementation
Make commands do work instead of planning work

final_verdict.summary: These commands are the software equivalent of a Rube Goldberg machine designed by someone who thinks complexity equals intelligence. You've created a beautiful, elaborate system for turning simple tasks into PhD dissertations.
final_verdict.bottom_line: LESS THINKING, MORE DOING. Your users want productivity tools, not philosophy engines.

methodology_notes.complexity_scoring: Based on CLAUDE.md rules: abstractions +2, patterns +3, configs +2
methodology_notes.bloat_calculation: Lines of actual logic vs. lines of thinking/templates/meta-stuff
methodology_notes.token_estimates: Based on typical Claude processing of structured markdown
methodology_notes.success_rates: Estimated based on complexity and user experience friction
